data.title: Ad Automation with Bürch: Step-by-Step Tutorial
data.excerpt: Learn how to set up Bürch (Revealbot) for Facebook, Google & Instagram ad automation. Save hours weekly with AI-powered rules and reporting.

$media.alt: Ad Automation with Bürch: Step-by-Step Tutorial

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*
I used to spend three hours every morning checking ad performance across Facebook, Google, and Instagram. Pausing underperformers. Bumping budgets on winners. Adjusting bids before breakfast.
Then I found
Bürch
(formerly Revealbot), and that routine shrank to ten minutes.

This tutorial walks you through exactly how to set up Bürch from scratch, configure your first automation rules, and start reclaiming your time. Whether you manage $2K or $50K per month in ad spend, the process is the same — and it's refreshingly simple.
What Is Bürch (Revealbot) and Why It Matters
Bürch is a
marketing automation platform
built specifically for paid media teams. It connects to your Facebook Ads, Google Ads, and Instagram accounts, then lets you create intelligent rules that run 24/7.
Think of it as an assistant that never sleeps: it monitors your campaigns, pauses ads that burn budget, scales winners automatically, and sends you clean reports without you lifting a finger.
The platform runs on AI-powered budget optimization, meaning it doesn't just follow rigid if/then logic. It learns from your campaign patterns and makes smarter decisions over time.

Pricing starts at $49/month for ad spend up to $10K, with an annual option that drops it to $45/month (plus one month free). No overage fees on the Essential plan — a detail that surprised me positively.
Start your free trial of Bürch here
Step 1: Create Your Account and Connect Ad Platforms
Time needed: 5–10 minutes
Head to
bir.ch
and click "Try for free" on the pricing page. You'll land on the signup screen — no credit card required for the trial.
Once inside the dashboard:
Navigate to Integrations
in the left sidebar.
Connect Facebook Ads:
Click "Add Account," log in with your Facebook credentials, and select the ad accounts you want Bürch to manage.
Connect Google Ads:
Same process — authorize via OAuth and pick your accounts.
Connect Instagram:
This flows through your Facebook Business Manager, so if you already connected Facebook, Instagram is one click away.
Bürch pulls in your historical data immediately. You'll see your campaigns, ad sets, and individual ads populate within minutes.
Pro tip:
Connect all ad accounts under one Bürch workspace. The pricing is based on total monthly ad spend across connected accounts, so bundling gives you better value.
Step 2: Build Your First Automation Rule
Time needed: 10–15 minutes
This is where Bürch earns its keep. Let's build a rule that pauses any Facebook ad set with a cost-per-result 40% above your target.
Go to Automations → Create Rule.
Select your ad level:
Choose "Ad Set" (you can also target campaigns or individual ads).
Set your condition:
`Cost per Result > $35` (adjust to your target CPA).
Add a secondary condition (optional):
`Impressions > 1,000` — this prevents pausing fresh ad sets before they have enough data.
Choose your action:
"Pause Ad Set."
Set the schedule:
Every 15 minutes is a solid starting point.
Name your rule
something clear: "Pause High CPA Ad Sets (>$35)."
Hit Save.
That's it. From this moment, Bürch watches your account around the clock.
Want the opposite behavior too? Create a companion rule: "Scale Ad Sets with CPA < $20" → increase daily budget by 20%. Now you have a self-optimizing feedback loop.
See more automation ideas and use cases
Step 3: Set Up Budget Optimization with AI
Time needed: 10 minutes
Bürch's AI budget optimization is a standout feature. Instead of static rules, you define your goals and let the algorithm redistribute spend across campaigns in real time.
Here's how to configure it:
Go to Budget Optimization
in the dashboard.
Select your campaigns
(group them by objective — e.g., "Prospecting" or "Retargeting").
Set your daily budget range:
Minimum and maximum spend per campaign.
Define your optimization goal:
Conversions, ROAS, cost per purchase — whatever matters most.
Enable AI redistribution:
Bürch shifts budget toward the campaigns performing best *right now*, not yesterday.
I've watched this single feature improve ROAS by 15–25% for campaigns that were previously managed manually. The AI reacts faster than any human checking dashboards every few hours.
If you're running simultaneous campaigns across Facebook and Google, Bürch handles both from the same optimization engine. No switching tabs.
Explore all the ways Bürch can automate your workflow
Step 4: Configure Alerts and Reporting
Time needed: 5–10 minutes
Rules handle the automatic actions. Alerts handle the "hey, look at this" moments.
To set up alerts:
Go to Automations → Create Alert.
Pick your metric:
Spend spike, CTR drop, frequency too high, delivery issues — Bürch covers all the signals.
Choose your channel:
Email, Slack, Telegram, or a webhook.
Set thresholds:
Example: "Notify me if daily spend exceeds $500 on any single campaign."
For reporting:
Bürch's reporting dashboard pulls real-time data from all connected platforms into one clean view. You can:
Build custom dashboards with drag-and-drop widgets.
Schedule automatic report delivery to clients or stakeholders (daily, weekly, monthly).
Filter by platform, campaign type, date range, or custom labels.
One feature I love:
cross-platform comparison views.
You see Facebook, Google, and Instagram metrics side by side, normalized to the same KPIs. No more opening three separate ad managers.
New and Highlighted Features Worth Knowing
Bürch continues to ship improvements regularly. Here are some features that stand out right now:
Multi-condition rule stacking:
Combine 5+ conditions in a single rule for surgical precision. Example: pause ads only when CPA is high AND frequency exceeds 4 AND the ad has run for 3+ days.
Time-of-day scheduling:
Rules can behave differently at different hours. Scale budgets during peak conversion windows and pull back at night.
Slack and Telegram integration:
Get real-time alerts directly in your team channels. No more checking email for critical updates.
Bulk rule management:
Duplicate, edit, or pause hundreds of rules at once. Essential if you manage multiple clients or product lines.
Historical performance snapshots:
Bürch stores rule execution history so you can audit what changed, when, and why.
Read the full breakdown of Bürch's newest features
Who Is Bürch Ideal For?
Bürch isn't just for agencies with massive budgets. Here's who gets the most value:
Solo media buyers
managing their own e-commerce or SaaS ads — you get enterprise-level automation without the enterprise price tag.
Small to mid-size agencies
handling 5–30 client accounts — the multi-account dashboard and scheduled reports save enormous time.
Performance marketers
who run Facebook, Google, *and* Instagram simultaneously — unified management is the whole point.
Growth teams at startups
with lean headcount — Bürch acts like a fractional media ops hire.
Anyone tired of manual ad monitoring
— if you've ever lost money because a bad ad ran overnight, Bürch eliminates that problem entirely.
The Essential plan at $49/month covers ad spend up to $10K, which fits most small-to-mid budgets comfortably. Scaling up is seamless — pricing tiers adjust based on your total connected spend.
Find out if Bürch is the right fit for your stack
Tips and Best Practices for Getting the Most Out of Bürch
After using Bürch across multiple accounts, here's what I recommend:
Start simple.
Don't build 50 rules on day one. Begin with 3–5 core rules (pause losers, scale winners, cap frequency) and expand from there.
Use the "dry run" mode.
Before activating any rule, run it in simulation to see what it *would have* done. This prevents accidental pauses on good campaigns.
Set alert fatigue boundaries.
Configure minimum time between repeated alerts for the same issue. Otherwise, your Slack will blow up.
Group campaigns by objective.
Bürch's AI optimization works best when it compares apples to apples. Don't mix prospecting and retargeting in the same optimization group.
Review rule performance weekly.
Check the execution log to make sure rules are firing as expected and not creating unexpected side effects.
Combine Bürch with other tools.
If you use
Manychat
for conversational flows or
n8n
for workflow automation, Bürch fits right alongside them in your stack.
Get more tips and advanced strategies
Final Thoughts: Why I Recommend Bürch Without Hesitation
Ad platforms are powerful, but their native automation is limited. Facebook's rules are clunky. Google's scripts require coding. Instagram doesn't offer much at all.
Bürch fills that gap with a clean interface, fast setup, and AI that actually improves results over time. The free trial makes it risk-free to test.
If you're spending even a few thousand dollars per month on ads, the time you save — and the wasted spend you prevent — will pay for the subscription within the first week.
Try Bürch free and automate your first rule today
Frequently Asked Questions
Does Bürch work with all Facebook campaign types?
Yes. Bürch supports all Facebook Ads campaign objectives including conversions, traffic, lead generation, video views, and dynamic ads. It also covers Advantage+ campaigns.
Can I use Bürch for multiple clients or ad accounts?
Absolutely. You can connect multiple ad accounts under a single workspace, making it ideal for agencies. Pricing is based on total monthly ad spend across all connected accounts.
What happens if I exceed my plan's ad spend limit?
The Essential plan includes clear overage policies. On annual billing, no overage fees apply. You can also upgrade to a higher tier at any time.

children.text: Does Bürch (Revealbot) work with both Facebook and Google Ads?
Yes. Bürch connects to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, and Instagram through a single dashboard. You can create automation rules and AI budget optimization across all three platforms simultaneously.
How much does Bürch cost for a small business?
The Essential plan starts at $49/month (or $45/month billed annually) for ad spend up to $10K. Annual billing includes one free month and no overage fees.
Can I try Bürch before committing to a paid plan?
Yes. Bürch offers a free trial with no credit card required. You can connect your ad accounts, build rules, and test the platform risk-free before subscribing.
